g.data-narration: Welcome to JSP Control Statements - learning dynamic web content generation
g.text: Dynamic Content Generation in Java Server Pages

g.data-narration: JSP control flow enables dynamic HTML generation based on conditions like request parameters and session data
g.text: JSP control statements embed Java-like structures in HTML to generate dynamic content based on:

g.data-narration: JSP provides three ways to implement control flow: traditional scriptlets, expression language, and JSTL tags
Scriptlets use three delimiter types: declarations for methods, expressions for output, and scriptlets for code blocks
Conditional rendering in scriptlets checks session attributes and displays different HTML based on user type
Scriptlet loops iterate over collections like inventory lists, embedding HTML table rows for each item
JSTL provides XML-style tags for control flow, requiring taglib declaration with core and functions URIs
JSTL conditional tags include c:if for simple tests and c:choose for multiple conditions with c:when and c:otherwise
g.text: JSTL Conditional Tags - c:if and c:choose

g.data-narration: JSTL iteration tags include c:forEach for loops and c:forTokens for parsing delimited strings
g.text: JSTL Iteration Tags - c:forEach and c:forTokens

g.data-narration: JSP provides nine implicit objects including request, response, session, and out for web-specific operations
g.text: Implicit Objects in JSP Control Flow

g.data-narration: This practical example shows role-based access control, checking session for user authentication and admin privileges
This JSTL pagination example demonstrates c:set for variables and c:forEach with begin/end for page numbers
JSTL outperforms scriptlets in readability, maintainability, and separation of concerns - making it the modern choice
Follow these best practices: prefer JSTL over scriptlets, use MVC pattern, and leverage EL for simple conditions
g.text: Best Practices for JSP Control Flow

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: scriptlets use Java syntax in JSP, JSTL provides clean XML tags, and implicit objects enable web operations
Remember: scriptlet delimiters, JSTL core tags, implicit objects, directives, and EL syntax for exam success
